summary: If You Break a block above you and Press Q it ll dupe the item your holding
description: If you break a block above you, Press Q and drop the item your holding at a certain time frame it ll drop your item while a duped one stays in your hand and if you switch to an empty hot bar slot you can pick it up (if you pick it up with the duped item in your hand it ll disappear) I m not sure about the timing for pressing Q but after a while something caused my duped Netherite pickaxe to disappear n n n n
Steps to Reproduce:
1. Create a new Survival world with default settings.
2. Obtain a Netherite Pickaxe and ensure it is in your main hand.
3. Find a solid block directly above your character (such as Stone or Dirt).
4. Break the block above you by holding the left mouse button until it is destroyed.
5. Immediately after breaking the block, press the Q key to drop the item you are holding (the Netherite Pickaxe).
6. Observe that the item drops while a duplicate remains in your hand.
7. Switch to an empty hotbar slot (make sure you do not have any items in that slot).
8. Pick up the dropped item from the ground.
9. If you attempt to pick up the dropped item while holding the duplicate, notice that the duplicate disappears. 
10. Wait for a period of time to see if the duped item in your hand eventually disappears.
Observed Behavior:
When a player breaks a block directly above their head and immediately presses the "Q" key to drop the item they are holding, the game exhibits a duplication bug. Specifically, the following sequence occurs:

1. The player breaks a block above them, which triggers the block-breaking animation and drops the block item.
2. Upon pressing "Q" at the right moment (the timing is crucial but not precisely defined), the item currently held by the player is dropped, while a duplicate version of that item remains in the player's hand.
3. If the player then switches to an empty slot in their hotbar, they can pick up the dropped item. However, if they attempt to pick it up while still holding the duplicated item, the newly acquired item will disappear.
4. This behavior can lead to the player having a duplicated item in their hand, while the original item is dropped on the ground.
5. Additionally, there seems to be a time limit after which the duplicated item may disappear, although the exact conditions leading to this disappearance are unclear.

This bug has been observed specifically with items such as a Netherite pickaxe, which can become lost if not picked up correctly after duplication. The mechanics of timing and item management are crucial to reproducing this bug successfully.
Expected Behavior: When a player breaks a block that is directly above them, the expected behavior is that the item currently held in their hand should drop normally without duplicating. If the player presses the Q key to drop the item after breaking the block, the game should not allow any duplication of the item. The item should drop into the world, and the player's hand should reflect the correct item count. If the player then switches to an empty hotbar slot, they should not be able to pick up any items that were not legitimately dropped; any item that was dropped should be picked up correctly without any disappearing or duplication effects occurring. Additionally, if the player attempts to pick up a dropped item while holding a duplicate, the game should correctly handle the situation without causing the dropped item to vanish. The mechanics of item dropping and inventory management should function as outlined in the official game documentation, ensuring a consistent and expected experience for players.

comments.body_text:
*We do not have enough information to find the cause of this issue.*

Please **record a video of this happening** and attach it to this report.If you are on Windows, you can use `Windows`+`Alt`+`R` to open a built-in app for recording game footage.If you are on Mac (Mojave or later), you can use `Shift`+`Command`+`5` to open a built-in app for recording your screen.In case you don't have a program to record videos, we recommend using the free recording software OBS.In case the resulting video file is too large to be uploaded to the bug tracker directly, please upload it elsewhere (e.g. as unlisted video on YouTube) and link to it here.

This issue is being temporarily resolved as **Awaiting Response**. Once the requested information has been delivered, the report will be reopened automatically.

**Quick Links**:📓 Issue Guidelines – 💬 Mojang Support – 📧 Suggestions – 📖 Minecraft Wiki
comments.rendered_body:
We do not have enough information to find the cause of this issue.

Please record a video of this happening and attach it to this report.
If you are on Windows, you can use Windows+Alt+R to open a built-in app for recording game footage.
If you are on Mac (Mojave or later), you can use Shift+Command+5 to open a built-in app for recording your screen.
In case you don't have a program to record videos, we recommend using the free recording software OBS.
In case the resulting video file is too large to be uploaded to the bug tracker directly, please upload it elsewhere (e.g. as unlisted video on YouTube) and link to it here.

This issue is being temporarily resolved as Awaiting Response. Once the requested information has been delivered, the report will be reopened automatically.

Quick Links:
📓 Issue Guidelines – 💬 Mojang Support – 📧 Suggestions – 📖 Minecraft Wiki
